items.user_turn: I'm planning a Paris trip from April 10–18, 2026 (flexible by a couple of days). It's two adults (34 and 33) and a 2-year-old; one adult needs wheelchair-accessible entry, so accessibility and stroller policies are important. We'll be staying at Hotel Le Petit Louvre, 12 Rue de l'Arbre Sec, 75001 Paris, phone +33 1 42 61 00 00, reservation under Alex Morgan — the hotel's central location should help prioritize nearby museums and cut down transit with a toddler. I prefer to pay no more than €25 per adult ticket and I don't have a Paris Museum Pass yet. Please produce a prioritized booking plan (which museums to reserve first), check current availability and alternative date/time strategies, and set up a monitoring/reminder plan so I can secure slots without last-minute scrambling.

items.user_turn: Please help me find and hire a reliable visa service for an Indian tourist visa. Passport: Full name Samantha Lee, Passport number N12345678, Nationality United States. Travel dates: depart 2026-06-15, return 2026-07-02. Itinerary: Delhi → Agra → Jaipur. Contact and pickup: email samantha.lee@example.com, phone 555-987-6543, preferred pickup address 45 Maple St, Apt 3B, Springfield, IL 62704. My maximum budget is $180; I prefer processing within 7 business days but can pay for a 48-hour rush if necessary. I want reputable providers compared by cost and turnaround, an exact list of documents I must give them and how to provide them securely, and a simple checklist and booking steps so I can avoid embassy queues.

items.user_turn: Help me create a compact, practical plan to communicate across three countries on an upcoming itinerary: Spain — Madrid (May 1–3, 2026), France — Paris (May 4–7, 2026), and Italy — Rome (May 8–10, 2026). I have a severe peanut allergy and am allergic to shellfish (I carry an EpiPen), so I need clear medical and allergy wording translated and printable. I can spend up to $50 on apps or resources and have about 500 MB of mobile data per day, so I prefer offline-first solutions. Please prioritize essential phrases by city and situation, build a small printable phrase sheet with audio (including emergency allergy wording), configure and test offline translation apps with saved-phrase shortcuts and audio playback, and suggest quick practice and fallback strategies.
